edges.description: Disorganized hair bundles and disrupted tip-links abolish the mechanotransduction current.
edges.source: Usher Interactome Disruption in the Hair Bundle

edges.description: The same Usher interactome is also required at the photoreceptor periciliary/connecting-cilium region, so loss of any Usher protein concurrently disrupts photoreceptor ciliary transport.
intermediate_mechanisms: shared Usher interactome required in both hair cells and photoreceptors
loss of Usher complex at the photoreceptor periciliary region
edges.source: Usher Interactome Disruption in the Hair Bundle

edges.description: Loss of cochlear hair cell transduction causes sensorineural hearing loss.
When the Usher protein is required for hair-bundle development, transduction failure is present from birth, producing congenital sensorineural hearing loss (USH1/USH2).
Loss of vestibular hair cell transduction causes vestibular areflexia, most pronounced in USH1.
Congenital vestibular areflexia delays the acquisition of independent walking in USH1 infants.
Impaired ciliary transport and outer-segment maintenance cause progressive photoreceptor loss.
Progressive photoreceptor loss manifests clinically as retinitis pigmentosa.
Progressive peripheral photoreceptor loss constricts the visual field.
Photoreceptor dysfunction reduces or extinguishes the ERG response.

nodes.description: The electroretinogram is reduced or extinguished, reflecting widespread photoreceptor dysfunction, and is an early diagnostic finding even before symptomatic visual loss.

evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of USH2 is established in a proband using electrophysiologic and subjective tests of hearing and retinal function.

nodes.description: Cochlear implantation provides auditory rehabilitation for the severe-to-profound sensorineural hearing loss of USH1 and is most effective when performed early in childhood, typically before age one year.

evidence.snippet: Cochlear implantation should be considered as young as medically feasible, typically before age one year.

nodes.description: Congenital onset of sensorineural hearing loss is characteristic of USH1 and USH2.

evidence.snippet: Usher syndrome type I (USH1) is characterized by congenital bilateral profound sensorineural hearing loss, vestibular areflexia, and adolescent-onset retinitis pigmentosa (RP).

nodes.description: Progressive constriction of the peripheral visual field ("tunnel vision") results from peripheral photoreceptor loss.

evidence.snippet: progressive, bilateral, symmetric retinal degeneration that begins with night blindness and constricted visual fields (tunnel vision)

nodes.description: Children with USH1 have delayed independent walking secondary to congenital vestibular areflexia.

evidence.reference_title: Usher Syndrome: Genetics and Molecular Links of Hearing Loss and Directions for Therapy.
evidence.snippet: Usher syndrome (USH) is an autosomal recessive (AR) disorder that permanently and severely affects the senses of hearing, vision, and balance.

nodes.description: Disruption of the tip-link and hair-bundle architecture prevents deflection-gated opening of the mechanotransduction channel, so hair cells cannot convert sound-evoked or head-movement-evoked bundle deflection into a receptor potential. The result is sensorineural hearing loss and, in USH1, vestibular areflexia. The severity and onset (congenital vs progressive) depend on whether the protein is required for bundle development or maintenance.

evidence.reference_title: Usher Syndrome: Genetics and Molecular Links of Hearing Loss and Directions for Therapy.
evidence.snippet: Disease-causing mutations in USH genes can destabilize the tip links that bind the stereocilia to each other, and cause defects in protein trafficking and stereocilia bundle morphology, thereby inhibiting mechanosensory transduction.

nodes.description: Conventional amplification with hearing aids is used for the moderate-to- severe hearing loss of USH2 and early USH3.

evidence.snippet: Treatment of manifestations: Early fitting of hearing aids and speech training.

nodes.description: Nyctalopia (impaired vision in dim light) is an early symptom of the rod-predominant photoreceptor degeneration.

evidence.snippet: Retinitis pigmentosa (RP); progressive, bilateral, symmetric retinal degeneration that begins with night blindness and constricted visual fields (tunnel vision) and eventually includes decreased central visual acuity

nodes.description: Usher proteins are also expressed at the photoreceptor periciliary and connecting-cilium region, where they participate in protein trafficking between the inner and outer segments. Disruption of the Usher interactome here impairs periciliary structure and intersegmental transport, leading to photoreceptor stress and progressive degeneration that begins with rods (night blindness, peripheral field loss) and later involves cones.

evidence.reference_title: Usher protein functions in hair cells and photoreceptors.
evidence.snippet: Recent evidence linking photoreceptor cell dysfunction in the shaker 1 mouse model for Usher syndrome to light-induced protein translocation defects, combined with localization of an Usher protein interactome at the periciliary region of the photoreceptors suggests Usher proteins might regulate protein trafficking between the inner and outer segments of photoreceptors.

nodes.description: Progressive rod-then-cone photoreceptor degeneration produces the clinical picture of retinitis pigmentosa: nyctalopia and peripheral visual field constriction first, followed by central acuity loss. Fundus examination shows bone-spicule pigmentation and the electroretinogram becomes reduced or extinguished.

evidence.snippet: RP, a progressive bilateral symmetric degeneration of photoreceptors in the retina, develops in early adolescence, resulting in progressively constricted visual fields first, due to rod photoreceptor cell loss, followed by impaired visual acuity due to cone photoreceptor cell loss.

nodes.description: Progressive rod-cone dystrophy with bone-spicule pigmentary deposits, the ophthalmologic hallmark of Usher syndrome. Onset is prepubertal in USH1 and later (first-to-second decade) in USH2.

evidence.reference_title: Usher syndrome: clinical features, molecular genetics and advancing therapeutics.
evidence.snippet: Usher syndrome has three subtypes, each being clinically and genetically heterogeneous characterised by sensorineural hearing loss and retinitis pigmentosa (RP), with or without vestibular dysfunction.

nodes.description: Bilateral sensorineural hearing loss, congenital and severe-to-profound in USH1, congenital and moderate-to-severe (sloping, high-frequency-predominant) in USH2, and progressive/postlingual in USH3.

evidence.reference_title: Usher syndrome: clinical features, molecular genetics and advancing therapeutics.
evidence.snippet: Usher syndrome has three subtypes, each being clinically and genetically heterogeneous characterised by sensorineural hearing loss and retinitis pigmentosa (RP), with or without vestibular dysfunction.

nodes.description: The Usher proteins (myosin VIIa, harmonin, cadherin-23, protocadherin-15, sans, usherin, ADGRV1/VLGR1, whirlin, clarin-1) form an interconnected network that builds and maintains the cohesion of the stereocilia hair bundle in cochlear and vestibular hair cells. Cadherin-23 and protocadherin-15 constitute the tip-link that gates the mechanotransduction channel, while harmonin, sans, and myosin VIIa anchor the link complex and stabilize bundle architecture during development. Loss of function of any Usher gene disrupts hair-bundle cohesion and tip-link integrity.
nodes.id: Usher Interactome Disruption in the Hair Bundle

evidence.reference_title: Usher protein functions in hair cells and photoreceptors.
evidence.snippet: In mature hair cells, homodimers of the Usher cadherins, cadherin 23 and protocadherin 15, interact to form a structural fiber, the tip link, and the linkages that anchor the taller stereocilia's actin cytoskeleton core to the shorter adjacent stereocilia and the elusive mechanotransduction channels, explaining the deafness phenotype when these molecular interactions are perturbed.

evidence.reference_title: Usher syndrome: molecular links of pathogenesis, proteins and pathways.
evidence.snippet: The localization of the Usher proteins and the phenotype in animal models indicate that the Usher protein complex is essential in the morphogenesis of the stereocilia bundle in hair cells and in the calycal processes of photoreceptor cells.

nodes.description: Absent vestibular responses (vestibular areflexia) are characteristic of USH1 and cause delayed independent walking in infancy. Vestibular function is typically normal in USH2 and variable in USH3.

evidence.snippet: Usher syndrome type I (USH1) is characterized by congenital bilateral profound sensorineural hearing loss, vestibular areflexia, and adolescent-onset retinitis pigmentosa (RP).
